title: Connections, Channel Ownership, Heartbeats, and Recovery · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs connections, channel ownership, heartbeats, and recovery from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a cancellable connection and channel recovery state machine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use bounded long-lived AMQP connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, TLS, and lifecycle cost; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Assign channel ownership by publisher or consumer lifecycle and prevent concurrent confirm, acknowledgement, and topology operations from corrupting assumptions; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Choose a heartbeat timeout with network and flow-control evidence and distinguish heartbeat interval, TCP keepalive, and false-positive failure; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Long Lived Connection
steps.instruction:
Order the Long Lived Connection RabbitMQ and Go messaging evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Long Lived Connection.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Long Lived Connection assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Long Lived Connection, keep Long Lived Connection connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A review Go messaging team handles case 4762 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound heartbeat and recovery delay while rejecting stale channel-generation notifications; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use bounded long-lived AMQP connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, TLS, and lifecycle cost; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
